submission.title: Seeking Event Organizers/Promoters for a Creative Event in Berlin
submission.body:
Hey Berlin! 

I\`m a 23-year-old women putting together a creative event in Berlin and could use some advice from experienced event organizers or promoters. If you’re in the industry (or know someone who is), I’d love to connect! Whether it's a quick chat over the phone or grabbing a drink to bounce around ideas, your insights would be super helpful. 

Leave a comment or send me a DM if you’re up for it! Thanks so much, everyone! 🙌

comments.body: I know a few people in the scene, sent you a dm!
Amazing, thanks!!

submission.title: I sell digital products like ebooks - 7.5 months in
submission.body:
This is one of a few different stores I have. My digital products are ebooks and courses. I promote them mostly on social media like Instagram and Threads. All organic, no ads. 

I'm posting these because I am a work in progress. I want to keep learning. Experienced/seasoned sellers, can you give me some other ideas of other sales channels to pursue. I'm happy with my progress but, I want more. 

What else?

https://preview.redd.it/4k0fxqeq7tqd1.png?width=934&format=png&auto=webp&s=0f363c801a55dd3f632e4f8856aea31bca71d84c

You've done great already with no ads and all organic traffic. Although Ads bring more traffic, organic growth builds a community. Instagram is a great place to build a community. Tiktok is even better for community building in my opinion because of the high reach the algorithm provides. Search on Tiktok the niche of digital products similar to what you sell. See what videos do well, and adopt those ideas and video formats. If you create Tiktok videos to funnel customers to your Instagram page, that might bring more growth.   
  
Please share your Instagram page or whatever channels you use to share your products. I'd love to see your work and support!

That’s really great. Are your social media content faceless? It would be pretty helpful if you could share your strategy and what your ebook is about. 

Do DM me the link as well. Keep going!

Marketing your ebooks and courses can definitely be a bit of a challenge, but I've found a few tactics that really made a difference for me. First off, try leveraging communities where your target audience hangs out. Reddit, for example, can be a goldmine if you interact genuinely and offer value. Just dive into relevant subreddits, not with the intention to sell, but to help and share insights. The key here is to build trust first.

Email marketing also worked wonders for me. Once you've built a small following, consider collecting emails and sending out valuable content to nurture your audience.

For a more data-driven approach, you could also try something like Google Analytics to better understand where your audience comes from and what they're interested in. And for keeping an eye on Reddit discussions, Pulse social monitoring for Reddit provides targeted subreddit monitoring and intelligent comment drafting, which helped me engage with my audience more strategically.

You might also want to explore collaborations with other creators in your niche. It's always a win-win when you cross-promote each other's products or services. Stay active, responsive, and helpful, and you'll see the results. Good luck!

Take 3 most converting posts and target the users who didn't buy using automation or ads very cheaply.
Also, if you send the users into a funnel, even if it's organic traffic, you can 5X-8X your revenue because of a good funnel, and you make behavioral flow. Each action has a different offer.

Also, if you do an automatic rotator of messaging to people who saw your posts and went to the cart page or even just the site and didn't buy it, you will get sales.
Cost nothing, just time to build.

You can build this yourself, don't need anyone. It's just consistent testing. Dm me if you get stuck or need advice.

Building a following can take time, but you’re on the right track by trying new platforms. Consider playing around with different content types like short videos since they can grab attention quickly. You can use free tools like Canva to create engaging visuals for your social accounts.
Also, experimenting with platforms like TikTok for your courses can create a buzz due to its algorithm favoring new creators. Combine that approach with Pinterest where your audience might be searching for similar topics. For Reddit, if posting in some targeted subreddits, make sure to genuinely interact and maybe even offer free chapters or mini-courses to get people interested. You might also check out Tailwind for planning social media posts, while tools like Pulse social monitoring for Reddit can give insights into where your potential customers are hanging out on Reddit. Keep exploring and tweaking your strategy!

Getting frustrated is completely normal, especially when you’re juggling different platforms and trying to make it all work. I’ve been there too. Sometimes, focusing on just one or two platforms where you see the most engagement can help alleviate some of that pressure. It’s better to excel at one platform than stretch yourself too thin across many.
Automation tools can also be a lifesaver—tools like Buffer or Later can schedule posts in advance, so you’re not posting daily. It can free up your time for other strategies. Also, batch-creating content one day a week helps keep your feed consistent without the daily grind.
Remember, it’s okay to pivot your strategy if something isn’t working out. Maybe explore freelance gigs or part-time remote jobs to supplement income while increasing your digital product presence. Don’t be too hard on yourself; it’s a learning process. Keep experimenting and see what fits your style and schedule best. You’ve got this!
Thank you! Yes I think remote jobs would work, I thought about teaching online classes since I know english and its not my first language but idk 😭

submission.title: Party location in Berlin?
submission.body: Hi, I'm currently looking for a party location for my birthday. The group should be about 30 people. I'm a student so of course I'd prefer somewhere that's not too expensive. Maybe a bar that can accommodate that many people?

comments.body: M
A
T
R
I
Fitzgeraldo. Invite if you do it :)
X
This Matrix recommendation is ass you be better If you Hit Up a local bar and make something happen with the owner.
a friend needs exactly this information
Goodbye

* The Castle in Mitte has a backroom that can be privately booked. You can check with them—I believe it can accommodate around 25 people.
* Das Gift in Neukölln also has a room. You can message them on Instagram.
* Tante Lisbeth in Kreuzberg has a bigger space and sometimes accepts table reservations.
* Brewdog in Mariendorf, though a bit far, has a huge space too.
The Castle has space for that many and you can reserve easily on their website
The Castle also has a room for 30 and for 50 I believe

Having one symptom doesn't mean you have ADHD (or any disorder), you typically would haveany of the symptoms. Chest pain is a symptom of a heart attack, but if someone gets punched in the chest, their chest pain doesn't mean they are having a heart attack. 

That being said, churning through hobbies/ quickly getting bored of something is definitely one of the symptoms of ADHD. Having that alone doesn't mean someone has ADHD, but a lot of people with ADHD have that symptom.

submission.title: Fishing License
submission.body:
Hello, 

1) Does anyone know if it’s possible to obtain a fishing license if I don’t speak German yet? Can the test provided also in English? 

2) Is it worth it to obtain it if I am living in Berlin? Is there good fishing spots close by to the city? 

Thanks in advance!

comments.body: If you fish in Brandenburg you don't need a license. You just need to purchase a state permit (at any fishing store) and a permit for the river or lake where you want to fish. Total cost around 40 bucks per year give or take if I'm not mistaken. There are tons of places where you can fish in the surrounding area, basically any river or lake.

submission.title: Investování do nemovitostí
submission.body:
Ahoj, chtěl jsem koupit malou nemovitost pro diverzifikaci svého investičního portfolia. Bylo mi řečeno, že mnoho malých měst v Česku (Karviná, Přerov, Ústí nad Labem atd.) nabízí levné nemovitosti, které jsou dobrou investiční příležitostí. Mám pár dotazů a byl bych vděčný, kdyby mi někdo mohl pomoci:

Máte někdo zkušenosti s něčím podobným? Na jiných fórech jsem také četl, že trh s nemovitostmi v Česku je momentálně v hrozném stavu kvůli úrokovým sazbám?

Které z těchto měst podle vás mají největší růstový potenciál a vysokou poptávku? Zatím jsem si zkompiloval tato:
Ústí nad Labem
Chomutov
Bruntál
Teplice
Karviná
Slaný
Kladno
Most
Přerov

Znáte nějakého realitního agenta nebo agenturu, která by mi mohla pomoci vybrat nejlepší město pro koupi nemovitosti? Všichni realitní makléři, se kterými jsem mluvil, se zaměřují pouze na Prahu.

Předem díky za vaši pomoc, moc si toho vážím!

comments.body: Mám zkušenost s pronájem bytu, kdy je dost možné, že díky exekucím, neslušným lidem atp, budeš mít zníčený majetek a dluhy na nájemném.  Náhoda je blbec ale pronajal jsem člověku co neměl nic a boom, za dva měsíce mu tam skočily exekuce, pronajal jsem známým od rodičů, boom prochcaný koberce od psa, strhané žaluzie, smrad od cigaret. Za mě to je docela risk..... A na realitní kanceláře jsem měl taky smůlu, při koupi zvýhodnovali prodávajícího, následně při prodeji kupujícího. Na lidi za mě není spoleh.
Did you use Google Translate or ChatGPT?
Lidi jsou nejhorší

Jakákoliv investice má riziko, rizikovější oblasti jsou v prvním pohledu zajímavější, ale rizikovější ... je to tak se vším ... větší riziko = větší možnost výdělku ... No a obecně nemovitosti jdou na nějakých \~10 let návratnosti ... S&P500 s průměrným růstem 10% je na tom velmi podobně ca 7 let. ...
Nejhorší jsou zmetci co chtějí dělat byznys z bydlení.

Hey siri, play Red sun in the sky

https://preview.redd.it/4bfrkylff5vd1.jpeg?width=1280&format=pjpg&auto=webp&s=0ddf02fc9a1ebc29966d9dc18518e3460b48da16

Neměl jsem to jako byznys, koupil jsem byt, několik let v něm bydlel, pak jsem šel o město dále za prací ale stále v rozumné vzdálenosti, kdy jsem každých 14 dnů navštěvoval rodinu. Byt jsem následně prodal, když jsem se odstěhoval doslova na opačný konec republiky, kde jsem nemohl mít přehled o stavu bytu. Byt jsem doslova pronajímal za náklady a hypotéku si nadále platil.   
Navíc nadávaní na lidi co dělají byznys bydlením, byt si může koupit každý, že na to hroamda lidí nemá je jejich problém.  
Taky nyní bydlím v pronájmu, protože na byt tady již nemám, to neznamená, že budu nasraný na lidi co na byty mají.  
Nedávno jsem v práci slyšel, že lidi by neměli brát příspěvky na bydlení a pokud na bydlení nemají, mají se odstěhovat tam kde na to budou mít, ten samej vám ale pak řekne, že nesnáší, že mu ve vedlejším bytě bydlí 2 ukrajinské rodiny (celkem 6 lidí). Paradox a pokrytectví lidí nemá konce.

Jo,kup aspoň přijdeš o peníze do toho si rovnou kup sp500.
V Nizozemsku vůbec neplatí daňňe z nemovitosti ( z nerealizované hodnoty)a unás to nikdy nebude.
Kupuj te všichni nemovitosti

Lidi hlavně nevidí za roh, hlavně hodně hamty hamty, ti samí čuráci pak budou nadávat, že jim nemá kdo vydělat na důchod, až jim všechny ty byty vybydlej. 


Příspěvek na bydlení je opravdu naprostá hovadina, která jen přispívá k současné neudržitelné systémové bublině.


Paradoxně jediný, komu současnost vyhovuje jsou na šedé politické eminence napojení šíbři a spekulanti, nikomu jinému. Lidi nechápou, že se jen zvyšuje cenová hladina ve finále, masivně na tom vydělá jen pár těch největších sviní, které nekupují byty, ale baráky.


Ale to je fakt marný řešit.
Protože jinak se bydlení pronajímat nedá.

Zkusenost mam, verejne psat nechci a ohledem na to, ze me dle toho lze do znacne miry lokalizovat, coz si nepreju. Pokud chces, posli DM, muzu poskytnout nejaky rough cisla jak to vychazelo za dobu vlastnictvi a i dat tip na spravce a maklere v konkretni lokalite.

Also, I understood from your profile that you are not Czech? English is also fine, better than google translate/chatgpt.
jsem v podobné situaci a upřímně se bojím doby kdy ho začnu pronajímat, asi pozvu nějakého stbáka aby mi zajemce předem proklepl (bezesrandy)
Well yes, all of them are places people do not want to live due to less than pleasant neighbors. That's why they are cheap.
Je to tak, v řadě měst je cena nemovitostí nepoměrně nižší než je tomu ve zbytku republiky. Ale není to náhoda. Jsou to místa, kde nechceš žít a už vůbec ne pronajímat.
Kup garáž v nějaké zajímavé lokalitě a tu pronajmi. Je s tím nejmíň práce a i daleko lépe se to prodává.
Chceš si koupit byt na investici ale jsi naprosto neschopný si i jen najít RK co operuje mimo Prahu, LOL. M&M reality třebas, co? Nic? Eeeh, vyser se na to.
Cikánov? Ano, tam jsou nemovitosti levné. Ale proč?
Energie samozřejmě přepsat, po ukončení podnájmu double check, že tam nemá hlášené bydliště - jinak úředně zrušit. Samozřejmostí jsou 2-3 nájmy jako kauce na tyhle případy. Pronajímat holobyt.
MM reality doporucujes vazne, nebo jako vtip? 😁

Neříkám ať přes ně nakupuje, ale jako nechápu že není schopný najít někoho kdo operuje mimo Prahu. Samo že RK jsou tuny, lokální i celostátní.

BTW vždy je to otázka nabídky a poptávky při nějakých dohodnutých podmínkách, nevidím důvod M&M hned zatracovat.

No, tak moje zkusenost s nimi je takova, ze jakekoliv ustupky od jejich smlouvy (pro kupce dost nevyhodne) nebyly mozne. Prisla jsem diky tomu o fajn nemovitost za dobrou cenu. Od te doby jiz na inzeraty MM ani nevolam.
Jo tak je pravda že s nimi nemám zkušenost jako kupující, pouze opačnou, a tu nemám negativní, spíše naopak.
I did yes!

Hele za mě v pohodě i takových lokalitách. Nemovitost si spíš najdi sám poblíž dopravního uzlu. Tady v Čechách je to samý podvodník, co ti prodá i svou mámu. Pak s nájemníky to taky jde. Lidi si prověřím, jak jen to jde. Dívám se na to kde pracuje, co je to za firmu, jak se projevuje apod. Jakože to holt musíš dělat, tak jak Němci. Tam když si chceš něco pronajmout, tak po tobě chtějí tolik lejster jako banka. Zálohy si říkám dva nájmy. Byty jsem ale stejně rekonstruoval, tak že jsou docela robustní. Taky existovala možnost, aby lidi odsouhlasili, že půjdou rovnou do exekuce, když nezaplatí nájem. Jmenuje se to svolení k vykonatelnosti a jako strašák super.

submission.title: Real estate for portfolio diversification
submission.body:
Hello, I wanted to purchase a small property to diversify my investment portfolio. I was told that some of the small towns around Czech (Karviná, Přerov, Usti nad Labem, etc.) offer cheap properties that are a good investment opportunity. I have a few inquiries, and would be grateful if anyone could help: 1) Does anyone have any experience doing something like this? I have also read on other sub that the real estate market in Czech is terrible at the moment because of the interest rates? 

2. Is there a better RE market/country to invest in? These small houses usually cost around 80k, so it’s relatively affordable, but I can also opt for a bigger property (400k)? I am mostly thinking about long-term rentals with the aim of portfolio diversification? 
Thanks in advance for your help, much appreciated!

comments.body:
All investments have risk, but I assume you have the 80k in cash? 

I personally do not like rentals as I have to manage them, or pay a rental company to do it properly that take a fee. 

However, if you still need help / advice on rental property from Reddit, maybe not do it.

>Does anyone have any experience doing something like this?

Yes but in Austria. Terrible market in czech rn due to interest rates? Rates are solid atm still - same for all of europe (thanks, ECB), not *golden* like 0.5% anymore either tho. The market itself, can be terrible, but I cannot comment on that. In Austria, it certainly *is* terrible right now and I would not touch any just yet..

>Is there a better RE market/country to invest in?

For the sake of maintenance, taxes and other problems when buying longterm rentals outside your close reach, dont do that really.. stay in czech - close to your own place, or let it be. Only exception I would make, is when you buy a bigger property and let an external company do all the dirty work - that requires some experience with RE beforehand tho else I wouldnt recommend it either..

>I am mostly thinking about long-term rentals with the aim of portfolio diversification?

For diversification - sure..

Just be sure that you 1) understand the work that goes into Rentals - its not 100% hands off 2) understand all taxes that come along with it and factored that in 3) you dont rely 100% on the income of those said rentals 4) get at least 5% gross income from rent only (not factoring in any property appreciation)

The last part is super important for me personally..
Why? 5% gross means you may get 4% a year net which means a 25y RoI which already sucks if we are honest, but thats all for diversification, right? (And the little hope prices are doubling again in 10y time..)

If its yielding any less, I would personally not even think about it.. any other fully passive investment is just so much better in terms of yields then

Gl!

Not the price of a house is the most important.

It’s location, location, location.

‘Buy the worst house in the best location’ and improve it.

In a good location:

- it’s easier to rent out (at a better price)

- it’s easier to resell 

- appreciation over time will be larger
re your final point - not sure about the Czech Republic, but in Poland tax on rental income is a flat 8.5%.

watch out, buying small propriety does the exact opposite of "diversify your portfolio"

It actually concentrates your portfolio quite a bit on a single very specific asset. If you want to include real estate to actually diversify your portfolio, buying REITs is a smarter idea

I'm not saying it's necessarily a bad idea, just that if your goal is diversification, then it's a horrible idea

submission.title: Real estate for portfolio diversification
submission.body:
Hello, I wanted to purchase a small property to diversify my investment portfolio. I was told that some of the small towns around Czech (Karviná, Přerov, Usti nad Labem, etc.) offer cheap properties that are a good investment opportunity. I have a few inquiries, and would be grateful if anyone could help: 1) Does anyone have any experience doing something like this? I have also read on other sub that the real estate market in Czech is terrible at the moment because of the interest rates? 

2. Is there a better RE market/country to invest in? These small houses usually cost around 80k, so it’s relatively affordable, but I can also opt for a bigger property (400k)? I am mostly thinking about long-term rentals with the aim of portfolio diversification? 
Thanks in advance for your help, much appreciated!

comments.body:
Why not just buy REITs instead.

Do you really want to deal with tenants, leaking roofs, mold, broken pipes, unpaid bills, insurance etc.

Real estate is a potentially highly profitable investment if you're ready to put the work in. That being said, although I'm not a Czech real estate expert, I know the country well enough to be skeptical about investing in smaller towns, most of which have been continuously declining in population. Prague has an extremely hot RE market and is likely going to continue to be one of the only real growth markets in the Czech Republic
Do you have some example good ones?

Realty Income, ticker O.

Right now starting yield of 4.99%, paying monthly (like rent) and keeps increasing (by a fraction but never goes down). So basically for each stock you own you get $0.26 each month in dividends. Price of \~$60 (it was around $50 just couple months back and then the starting yield was \~6%). They have never missed a payment in last 54 years!

Quote from their website [https://www.realtyincome.com/](https://www.realtyincome.com/) :

"Realty Income (NYSE: O), an S&P 500 company, is real estate partner to the world's leading companies. Founded in 1969, we invest in diversified commercial real estate and have a portfolio of 15,450 properties in all 50 U.S. states, the U.K. and six other countries in Europe. We are known as "The Monthly Dividend Company^(®)," and have a mission to deliver stockholders dependable monthly dividends that grow over time. Since our founding, we have declared 652 consecutive monthly dividends and are a member of the S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats^(®) index, having increased our dividend for the last 30 consecutive years."

Buy there are various ones like MAIN, VICI, IRM, SLG or even ETFs that hold a bunch of REITs like VNQ.

The REIT isn't doing it for free though, theres plenty of management cost overhead involved you're paying for. You're also not going to be able to leverage your capital invested into REITs like you can when you actually own the properties. Theres a reason why theres plenty of real estate millionaires but no one of them is going to be telling you they got there by buying REITs, if you're willing to put in the work and think of it as a job you're doing, you are the one reaping all the rewards as well, obviously you're also taking all the risks as well.

However, if you have to make questions like this in Reddit like OP did, I'd steer clear from real estate in the first place.

Hey. I do have that experience, but from large city. Been renting out 2 apts - one for short-term airbnb (technically, it's a business, not passive income), one for long-term 'classic' rental (was lucky enough to start renting it out after renovation, then had only reliable tenants without major issues, last one for 5+ yrs).

I can't comment on smaller cities like the ones you mention. The purchase price might be sound, my one question would be A) ability to get reliable tenants (pay, don't destroy the property, don't annoy neighbors, etc.), B) can you make it low-effort but high enough to be interested?

To give a specific example from the long-term rental. The rent is just slightly above 3% of the property price (after tax-optimizations). If you include the property appreciation it's around 6%, but it's hard to predict if the prices will keep growing as during the last decade.

Ourselves, been thinking about diversifying outside CZ. Apts are overpriced so much, that you'd need to go for "panelak" or something slightly off central locations to make it worth it (again looking at % rent income).

  
Regarding interest rates, it's tricky. Historically, you could have 20% or even less in cash. Then get the rest as mortgage. And pay the monthly mortgage from the rental income. Very reasonable way. With the purchase prices being such high, the base you need to make this work is getting closer to 50%.

  
There are expensive options in AT, IT (alp ski resorts) that give you option to buy to let with 1-2 weeks a year for you to use the property. Fully managed. semi-guaranteed to return 6%, which nicely covers mortgage costs. Fully managed is the main benefit - to make it "feel" like stocks/bonds.

  
Last but not least, try looking at REITs, but do your research first..

"ready to put the work in" is where most people stop. having rental in portfolio isn't a problem, keeping it alive (maintenance, tenant management, taxes & accounting) requires some skill and time. so depends on what OP prefers. some skilled folks operate everything themselves and it's a nice "distraction" from daily routine. but it all works out until you have to put in more than 1hr/month per unit. then it becomes 2nd job.

  
my only worry is, that Prague prices are inflated and there are several reasons why it is so. Brno prices are inflated because of lack of new build ups. don't forget there are less-volume heavy people (born in 00s) so the demand might start to drop.. we'll see. some new apts are offered for 200k+/sqm. which is simply outrageous (given the price to build is \~100k/sqm).

Fees are way less than associated with the ownership - so that's just not true.

True is that you can't use leverage (borrowed money) as you can with buying a property.

REITs wont make you a millionaire but once you're there - they are great!  
Also watch out if you  strive becoming millionaire with borrowed/other people's money.

Fees are definitely not less than associated with ownership for someone who rents out few apartments and does all of the management by himself. Realty Income for example employs over 400 people including lawyers, corporate managers, HR, assistants, board members, analysts, property managers and so on and so on and so on. You definitely and absolutely do not need to account for that kind of running costs when you are just renting out a few apartments, and all of those costs will be deducted from the profits the REIT will return you.

You need all of these people at that scale.

Let's say you get to 20 doors - you won't need an property manager?  
Next level 45 doors - you think you won't need an accountant?
You’re already a multimillionaire at 20 doors in your name. You’re not a multimillionaire with ten thousand invested in a REIT.
Comparing apples to oranges.

First of all, I know multiple people managing their own rentals without a manager in the 20-30 scale. 

Second of all this is a fire sub, if you have 20+ rentals in your name you’re either overleveraged idiot or you’re already fire ready. 

Your apples to oranges argumentation is lack luster when its clear you cannot come up with a better one. Next argument will probably be diversification which is hilarious one in its own right. 

Its pretty clear you have no clue about real estate investing so REITs are probably an excellent choice for you to gain market exposure.

submission.title: Advice on Real Estate
submission.body:
Ahoj, chci koupit malou nemovitost jako investici do budoucna. Zistil jsem, že nemovitosti v mnohých malých městech v Česku jsou poměrne levné (Karviná, Přerov, Ústí nad Labem atd.). Mám pár otázek a byl bych vděčný za vaši pomoc.

Máte někdo zkušenosti s něčím podobným? 
Je vhodná doba pro koupu nemovitostí vzhledem na současnou situaci na trhu s nemovitostmi?
Které z těchto měst by byli dle vás nejvýhodnější? Zatím jsem našel tato:
Ústí nad Labem
Chomutov
Bruntál
Teplice
Karviná
Slaný
Kladno
Most
Přerov
Znáte nějakého realitního agenta nebo agenturu, která by mi mohla pomoci vybrat nejlepší město pro koupi nemovitosti? 

Všichni realitní makléři, se kterými jsem mluvil, se zaměřují pouze na Prahu.

Předem díky za vaši pomoc, moc si toho vážím!

comments.body:
Usti nad Labem for example, was a bigger city in the past, due to industrialisation, but is not as desirable to live now. Not that many jobs etc, although a train to Praha is still only 60 minutes.

It is hard to know what the price will do in 5,10,15 years. If you buy and have a tenant pay rent, that rent can pay your mortgage.
Or maybe find other ways of investing which won't create a bad impact on locals.
Lol Real estate in Karviná lol. Gypsies gonna tear wires out of walls and then smear it with feces
